title: Arrays & Hashing
description: The bedrock of all data storage. Fast access, fixed size, and key-value mapping.
tips: Master hash collisions and O(1) average lookup. Crucial for frequency counts.

title: Two Pointers
description: Iterating through data with multiple indices to optimize space and time.
tips: Usually reduces O(n^2) to O(n) for sorted arrays.

title: Sliding Window
description: Managing sub-arrays effectively by shifting a range over the data.
complexity: Time: O(n), Space: O(1) or O(k)

title: Stack
description: Last-In, First-Out. Modeling undo actions, brackets, and recursions.
tips: Watch out for O(N) patterns where elements are pushed once and popped once.

title: Binary Search
description: Divide and conquer. Half the search space every single time.
tips: Don't just search arrays; search the "answer" range.

title: Linked List
description: Dynamic memory usage. Nodes connected by pointers.
tips: Pointer manipulation is key. Always handle the NULL cases.

title: Trees
description: Hierarchical data. The root of many complex algorithms.
tips: Recursive thinking is mandatory here. Learn BFS and DFS.

title: Heap / Priority Queue
description: Finding the min or max in a collection constantly.
tips: Think "Top K" or "Kth Largest" whenever you see a heap.

title: Backtracking
description: Brute force with an exit strategy. Finding all possibilities.
tips: Visualize the decision tree to avoid infinite loops.

title: Graphs
description: The ultimate data structure. Nodes and edges mapping the world.
tips: Cycle detection and shortest paths are the bread and butter.

title: Dynamic Programming
description: Saving the future by remembering the past. Optimization at its finest.
tips: Find the recurrence relation first. Start small.
